confidence_rationale: Foundational NRN review documenting that biological neural systems are intrinsically stochastic from molecular to behavioural levels, and that the nervous system has co-evolved both noise-counter mechanisms AND noise-as-feature mechanisms (e.g., stochastic resonance, exploration). Key R1 evidence for the noise-arm: behavioural variability is partly an irreducible substrate property, not a separately-engineered exploration channel.
failure_signatures: An REE substrate that treats action selection as fully deterministic (no policy noise floor) will fail to reproduce the trial-to-trial variability biological agents exhibit even on highly-trained tasks.

If noise is the ONLY source of behavioural diversity (R1 noise-only verdict), this review would predict that all behavioural-diversity signatures could be reproduced by tuning a single temperature parameter -- which Wilson 2014 falsifies behaviourally.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Noise -- random disturbances of signals -- is intrinsic to nervous systems at every level (molecular, cellular, network, behavioural). It contributes to trial-to-trial variability in behaviour. The nervous system has both mechanisms to counter noise (averaging, redundancy) and mechanisms to exploit it (stochastic resonance, exploration).
mapping.ree_translation: ARC-065 MECH-313 (stochastic-exploration-floor candidate) has direct biological grounding: action-selection variability has an irreducible noise floor that an REE policy network needs to instantiate. The simplest implementation is non-zero softmax temperature on E3 candidate scoring + low-amplitude policy parameter perturbation. R1 verdict contribution: noise alone is insufficient (Wilson 2014 falsifies that), but a substrate that lacks a noise floor will fail to reproduce baseline behavioural variability.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The review establishes that noise is present and functional in biological systems; it does not directly test whether noise alone suffices for behavioural diversity in a decision-making sense. The mapping from cellular noise to action-policy stochasticity in REE is at a level of abstraction the paper does not directly address.
mapping.source_context: Nature Reviews Neuroscience review by Faisal (Cambridge, computational and biological learning lab), Selen, and Wolpert (Cambridge motor control). Cited >2000 times; canonical review.

confidence_components.notes: NRN venue + foundational status. Mapping fidelity moderate because the cellular-to-behavioural-policy bridge requires multiple inferential steps the paper does not explicitly traverse.
